SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.store.caffeine

Class FailoverStoreCaffeine<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.store.caffeine.FailoverStoreCaffeine<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the business payload held by each ReferentialPayload
All Implemented Interfaces:
FailoverStore<T>, FailoverStoreSizeAware
public class FailoverStoreCaffeine<T> extends Object implements FailoverStore<T>, FailoverStoreSizeAware
FailoverStore implementation backed by a single Caffeine in-memory cache.

Storage layout

All entries — regardless of referential name — are stored in one flat Cache. Each entry's cache key is the composite string "<name>##<key>", keeping name and key scoped together without a nested map structure.

Per-entry expiry

A custom Expiry policy derives each entry's TTL individually from its own ReferentialPayload expireOn timestamp at the moment of creation or update: 

   TTL = Duration.between(clock.now(), payload.getExpireOn())
This means two entries stored under the same referential name can have different TTLs and are evicted independently. Caffeine handles eviction automatically on a background thread, so cleanByExpiry(java.time.Instant) is a no-op for this implementation.

Read expiry

Reading an entry (find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String)) does not reset its TTL — the remaining duration is preserved unchanged on every read access.

Defensive copies

Both store(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>) and find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String) operate on copies of ReferentialPayload (ReferentialPayload.copy()). This prevents callers from mutating cached state through a retained reference.

Thread safety

Caffeine's Cache is fully thread-safe. All operations (store(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>), find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String), delete(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>)) may be called concurrently without external synchronisation.

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:

  • ReferentialPayload

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FailoverStoreCaffeine(FailoverClock fClock)
    Constructs an unbounded FailoverStoreCaffeine (size limited only by per-entry expiry).
    FailoverStoreCaffeine(FailoverClock fClock, long maximumSize)
    Constructs a FailoverStoreCaffeine with an optional maximum entry count.

  • Constructor Details

    • FailoverStoreCaffeine

      public FailoverStoreCaffeine(FailoverClock fClock)
      Constructs an unbounded FailoverStoreCaffeine (size limited only by per-entry expiry).
      Parameters:
      fClock - clock used to compute each entry's remaining TTL from its expireOn timestamp; must not be null

    • FailoverStoreCaffeine

      public FailoverStoreCaffeine(FailoverClock fClock, long maximumSize)
      Constructs a FailoverStoreCaffeine with an optional maximum entry count.

      When maximumSize > 0, Caffeine bounds the cache and evicts entries on a size-based (Window TinyLFU) policy once the cap is exceeded — capping heap use from high-cardinality keys. When maximumSize <= 0 the cache is unbounded and limited only by per-entry expiry (audit I-15).

      Parameters:
      fClock - clock used to compute each entry's remaining TTL from its expireOn timestamp; must not be null
      maximumSize - maximum number of entries to retain; <= 0 means unbounded

  • Method Details

    • store

      public void store(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
      Stores a defensive copy of the payload in the cache.

      The cache key is the composite "<name>##<key>". If an entry for that key already exists, it is replaced and its TTL is re-derived from the new payload's expireOn. If no entry exists, a new one is created with TTL computed as Duration.between(clock.now(), payload.getExpireOn()).

      Specified by:
      store in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to cache; must not be null

    • delete

      public void delete(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
      Invalidates the cache entry for the given payload.

      This is a no-op if the entry does not exist or has already been evicted by Caffeine.

      Specified by:
      delete in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to remove; must not be null

    • find

      public Optional<ReferentialPayload<T>> find(String name, String key)
      Looks up a payload by referential name and key.

      Returns a defensive copy of the cached payload if present and not yet evicted. Returns Optional.empty() if the entry was never stored, has expired, or was explicitly deleted. Reading an entry does not reset its TTL.

      Specified by:
      find in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      key - the unique key within that referential
      Returns:
      an Optional containing a copy of the cached payload, or empty if absent

    • findAll

      public List<ReferentialPayload<T>> findAll(String name) throws FailoverStoreException
      Returns defensive copies of all cached entries whose composite key is prefixed by name##.

      Performs a prefix scan over the live cache view (Cache.asMap()); already-expired entries are excluded by Caffeine. Returns an empty list when no entry matches.

      Specified by:
      findAll in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      Returns:
      defensive copies of all matching payloads, or an empty list if none match
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the underlying lookup operation fails

    • cleanByExpiry

      public void cleanByExpiry(Instant expiry)
      No-op for this implementation.

      Expiry is managed automatically by Caffeine's per-entry expiry policy; there is no need to scan and remove entries manually. The expiry argument is ignored.

      Specified by:
      cleanByExpiry in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      expiry - ignored

    • liveEntryCount

      public long liveEntryCount(String name)
      Counts the live cache entries currently held for the given referential name (prefix scan over Cache.asMap(); already-expired entries are excluded by Caffeine's live view).
      Specified by:
      liveEntryCount in interface FailoverStoreSizeAware
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      Returns:
      number of live entries cached under name
